content: My name is Alex. I am 33 years old and I was born on March 15th, 1992.
I live in San Francisco, California, in the Mission District neighborhood.
I work as a senior software engineer at Stripe, a fintech company focused on payments infrastructure.
I am fluent in Go and Python, and I also know JavaScript and Rust.
My favorite food is sushi, especially from a small Japanese restaurant near my apartment.
My hobbies include rock climbing, playing guitar, and reading science fiction novels.
I have a younger sister named Emma who recently graduated from Stanford University.
I also have a younger brother named David who is studying medicine at UCSF.
I went to MIT for college, where I studied computer science and graduated in 2014.
I have a cat named Luna. She is a tabby and I adopted her from a shelter in 2021.
Last year I traveled to Kyoto, Japan and visited the beautiful Fushimi Inari shrine.
I won the best paper award at NeurIPS 2023 for my research on efficient transformer architectures.
I am severely allergic to peanuts and always carry an EpiPen.
I bank with Chase and have been a customer since college.
I drive a Tesla Model 3 that I bought in 2022. I charge it at home using a Level 2 charger.
I use an iPhone 15 Pro as my primary phone.
My favorite movie is Inception by Christopher Nolan. I have seen it at least ten times.
I am currently reading Dune by Frank Herbert. It is my second time through the series.
My favorite band is Radiohead. OK Computer is my favorite album of all time.
I play tennis every Saturday morning at the Golden Gate Park courts.
I am 6 feet tall with dark brown hair and green eyes.
Every morning I drink an oat milk latte from Blue Bottle Coffee.
My favorite season is autumn, especially October in San Francisco.
I play acoustic guitar and performed at an open mic night once at a local bar.
My preferred IDE is VS Code with a dark theme and Vim keybindings.
I practice meditation for 20 minutes every morning before having coffee.
My favorite color is deep blue, which I painted my bedroom walls.
La Taqueria in the Mission District is my favorite restaurant. Their super burritos are amazing.
I work remotely most days, but go into the Stripe office on Tuesdays and Thursdays.
I used to live in Barcelona for two years and picked up fluent Spanish.
I prefer mechanical keyboards. I own three of them with different Cherry MX switches.
I love cooking Italian food, especially homemade pasta from scratch.
I completed my first half marathon in October 2023 with a time of 1:52:34.
My daily standup is at 9:30 AM Pacific time.
I run 5K in about 24 minutes and practice at Golden Gate Park on weekends.
I recently read The Pragmatic Programmer and found the advice on DRY principles valuable.
I prefer dark mode in all my editors and applications.
My favorite movie is The Shawshank Redemption. The ending always gives me hope.
I visited Tokyo last year and loved the ramen at Ichiran in Shibuya.
I attended a two-week Scala training course at my previous company in 2020.
I have 20/20 vision and do not need glasses.
I wake up at 6:30 AM every day, including weekends.
I listen to the Lex Fridman podcast regularly. The long-form conversations are excellent.
I gave a Go workshop at the SF Gophers meetup. About 40 people attended.
I am reading Designing Data-Intensive Applications by Martin Kleppmann.
I started a personal blog where I write about Go programming patterns.
Dolores Park is my favorite spot in San Francisco. The views of the skyline are stunning.
I am training for a marathon with a 16-week plan. My goal is sub-4 hours.
I prefer dark roast coffee from local roasters and brew it with a pour-over method.
I use Obsidian for note-taking and have over 500 notes linked together.
